elements.sent-2411-001: We classify the isoparametric hypersurfaces and the homogeneous hypersurfaces of $\h^n\times\R$ and $\s^n\times\R$, $n\ge 2$, by establishing that any such hypersurface has constant angle function and constant principal curvatures.
elements.sent-2411-002: The construction and classification of isoparametric hypersurfaces in general Riemannian manifolds has become a matter of great interest in submanifold theory since the early works by Cartan on this subject.
elements.sent-2411-003: In a series of four remarkable papers~\cite{cartan1 --\cite{cartan4 , published in the late 1930's, he classified the isoparametric hypersurfaces of the $n$-hyperbolic spaces $\h^n$, and brought to light that the classification of the isoparametric hypersurfaces of the $n$-spheres $\s^n$ is a rather intricate problem.}}
elements.sent-2411-004: As a matter of fact, this classification has been built through many works over the last decades, and only recently it was announced to be complete (see~\cite{cecil,chi ).}
elements.sent-2411-005: By definition, an isoparametric hypersurface has constant mean curvature, as do any nearby (locally defined) hypersurface which is parallel to it.
elements.sent-2411-006: Homogeneous hypersurfaces, that is, those which are codimension one orbits of isometric actions on the ambient space, are well known examples of isoparametric hypersurfaces.
elements.sent-2411-007: It turns out that all isoparametric hypersurfaces of Euclidean space $\R^n$ (classified by Segre~\cite{segre ), as well as those of hyperbolic space $\h^n$ (classified by Cartan~\cite{cartan1 ), are homogeneous.}}
elements.sent-2411-008: In $\s^n,$ one has the two types, homogeneous (classified by Hsiang and Lawson~\cite{HL ) and nonhomogeneous (cf.~\cite{FKM,ozeki-takeuchi ).}}
elements.sent-2411-009: Besides being isoparametric, homogeneous hypersurfaces have constant principal curvatures.
elements.sent-2411-010: However, the constancy of the principal curvatures implies neither being isoparametric nor homogeneous.
elements.sent-2411-011: For instance, in simply connected space forms, as proved by Cartan, a hypersurface is isoparametric if and only if it has constant principal curvatures.
elements.sent-2411-012: On the other hand, as we pointed out, there are nonhomogeneous isoparametric hypersurfaces in $\s^n$.
elements.sent-2411-013: In addition, Rodríguez-Vázquez~\cite{avazquez showed that, for each $n\ge 3$, there exists an $n$-dimensional torus which contains a non-isoparametric hypersurface whose principal curvatures are constant.}
elements.sent-2411-014: It should also be mentioned that there exist isoparametric hypersurfaces whose principal curvatures are not constant functions, as shown in~\cite{ramos-vazquez,geetal,GSS .}
elements.sent-2411-015: In~\cite{dominguezvazquez-manzano , Domínguez-Vázquez and Manzano classified the isoparametric surfaces of all simply connected homogeneous 3-manifolds with 4-dimensional isometry group.}
elements.sent-2411-016: These are the so called $\mathbb E(\kappa,\tau)$ spaces with $k-4\tau^2\ne 0$, which include the products $\h^2\times\R$ and $\s^2\times\R$.
elements.sent-2411-017: Their result also provides the classification of the homogeneous surfaces of these spaces, as well as of the surfaces having constant principal curvatures.
